behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler orchestration between signature verification and queue-forwarding side effect. MOCK-MISS: this is not just hash math; it must prove no enqueue on auth failure. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit because behavior text includes cross-module effect: "body is not forwarded." Rejected System because behavior text does not require real HTTP transport, only handler behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire HTTP handler + real verifier + in-memory/fake queue adapter; send request with invalid X-Signature-256 over in-process server, assert 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering/transaction boundary between handler response and queue write. MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests can miss await/order bugs across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local queue implementation (local DB/filesystem allowed at Integration); no real network/subprocess needed. Rejected System because behavior text requires write-before-ack ordering, not real transport faults.
behaviors.test_description: Start app with local persistent queue (e.g., sqlite/file-backed test instance), inject controllable queue write latency, issue request, and assert queue record exists before response completion timestamp/marker.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is async handoff boundary: enqueue/ack path must be decoupled from processor completion. REAL-INFRA: real OS/network not required; in-process wiring with slow processor stub reproduces true bug. Rejected Unit because behavior text depends on interaction with downstream processing module. Rejected System because no requirement for real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler, queue, and downstream worker where processing blocks (sleep/promise). Send valid event and assert response returns before worker completion signal.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets wall-clock latency under concurrency pressure, which depends on real runtime scheduling and HTTP serving behavior. MOCK-HIDE: fakes/timers can hide event-loop starvation, socket backlog, and contention effects. REAL-INFRA therefore required. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" under "slow and concurrent" conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process, create concurrent load with earlier slow events, then send a new valid webhook via real HTTP client and assert end-to-end response time <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at module/state boundary (handler + idempotency check + queue persistence). Unit-only logic tests can miss state handoff or repository constraint issues. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store is sufficient; no real network/subprocess required. Rejected Unit because behavior text is stateful across requests ("already received"). Rejected System because external infra is not required by wording.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the handler against test persistence; assert first request enqueues once and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is error propagation/mapping from queue module to HTTP response contract. MOCK-MISS: unit test of formatter alone misses integration of queue-full signal through handler path. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required; queue can be filled or forced to emit full condition in-process. Rejected System because behavior text is about semantic mapping, not real transport/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure queue to capacity (or force full error) in test wiring, send webhook, assert 503 and presence/value of Retry-After; assert no accepted enqueue.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component behavior against its real persistence-backed state/config, not just handler mapping. Minimum boundary is queue + persistence module interaction. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem persistence is enough (Integration definition). Rejected Unit because behavior text says "persistent queue actually enforces" (needs component wiring/state). Rejected System because no real external network/subprocess is required by behavior wording.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend in test mode with low capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection and unchanged stored count.
